Task 80 complete: Added get_activity_clusters_by_ids batch method to db.py, refactored merge route to eliminate N+1. Steps 1-3 wrote failing tests for the batch method, step 4 implemented it, step 5 wrote monkeypatch test proving batch path used, step 6 refactored the route. All 56 tests pass.

Write TestGetActivityClustersByIds.test_returns_dict_keyed_by_cluster_id — seed 3 clusters, fetch 2 by ID, assert dict has exactly 2 keys with correct data; Write TestGetActivityClustersByIds.test_returns_empty_dict_for_empty_input — call with [] and assert result is {}; Write TestGetActivityClustersByIds.test_omits_nonexistent_ids — seed c-1, fetch [c-1, nonexistent], assert dict has only c-1 key; Implement get_activity_clusters_by_ids in db.py after get_activity_clusters (~line 711). Mirror batch_delete pattern: early return {} for empty input, build placeholders, SELECT * WHERE cluster_id IN (...), return {row['cluster_id']: dict(row)} dict comprehension; Write TestApiMergeClustersUseBatchFetch.test_merge_uses_batch_fetch — monkeypatch db.get_activity_cluster to raise AssertionError('N+1 detected'), verify merge still succeeds (proving it uses the batch path, not the per-cluster path). Seeds 2 clusters via _seed_cluster_via_db, calls POST /api/clusters/merge; Refactor api_merge_clusters in review.py: replace lines 404-413 (per-cluster for loop) with a single call to db.get_activity_clusters_by_ids(body.cluster_ids), then check for missing IDs via set difference and raise 404 for the first missing ID. Convert dict values to list for the existing `clusters` variable
